preamble: The transformation from an open-mid front unrounded vowel (ɛ) to a nasalized close-mid front unrounded vowel (ẽ) is a documented phonetic shift involving both nasalization and a change in vowel height. This process typically occurs when an oral vowel becomes nasalized due to the influence of an adjacent nasal consonant, which can then trigger a raising of the vowel's articulation.

examples.note: In Horb Swabian, the oral open-mid front unrounded vowel /ɛ/ surfaces as the nasalized close-mid front unrounded vowel [ẽ] in contexts of regressive nasalization.

sources: Kniess, T. B. (2025). NASALIZATION AND NASAL VOWELS IN THE SWABIAN DIALECT OF HORB: A PHONOLOGICAL ANALYSIS OF KAUFFMANN 1890. IU ScholarWorks.
Evidence for vowel nasalization in early Greek in - Brill.

Effects of the Postvocalic Nasal on the Perception of American English Vowels by Native Speakers of American English and Japanese (Chapter 8) - Second Language Speech Learning - Cambridge University Press & Assessment.
